name: Formation of France
summary: West Francia began as a weak crown surrounded by powerful feudal duchies. Over seven centuries the Capetian and Valois kings absorbed Burgundy, Brittany, Aquitaine, Normandy and the rest into a centralised kingdom.

stages.label: West Francia — strong duchies, weak crown
Capetian kings expand the royal domain
Great fiefs absorbed into the crown

name: Rise of Russia
summary: From fragmented Rus principalities under Mongol overlordship, the Grand Duchy of Moscow 'gathered the Russian lands', threw off the Mongol yoke, and expanded into a Eurasian empire.

name: Unification of Spain
summary: The marriage of Isabella of Castile and Ferdinand of Aragon joined the two great Iberian crowns. The conquest of Granada in 1492 completed the Reconquista, forming the core of a unified Spain.

stages.label: Iberian kingdoms: Castile, Aragon, Navarre, Granada

name: Expansion of the United States
summary: From thirteen Atlantic colonies, the United States expanded westward through purchase, treaty and annexation until it spanned the continent coast to coast.

stages.label: Independence — 13 states on the Atlantic seaboard

name: Italian Unification (Risorgimento)
summary: After the Congress of Vienna, Italy was a patchwork of kingdoms, duchies and the Papal States. Led by Sardinia-Piedmont, the peninsula was unified into a single Kingdom of Italy, with Rome as capital from 1871.

name: German Unification
summary: The German Confederation was dozens of sovereign states. Prussia, through war and diplomacy under Bismarck, forged them into the German Empire, proclaimed in 1871.

name: Unification of China
summary: From the Warring States, the Qin forged the first unified Chinese empire. China cycled through unification, fragmentation and reunification under successive dynasties, down to the modern People's Republic.

stages.label: Qin & Han unify the warring states
Mongol Yuan rules all of China

name: Unification of Japan
summary: The Sengoku ('Warring States') period left Japan split among rival daimyō. Oda, Toyotomi and finally Tokugawa unified the islands; the Meiji Restoration then forged a centralised modern state.

name: Rise of the Ottoman Empire
summary: From a small Anatolian frontier principality, the Ottomans took Constantinople in 1453 and expanded across three continents to become the dominant power of the eastern Mediterranean.

name: Yugoslavia: Birth & Breakup
summary: South Slav lands divided between the Austro-Hungarian and Ottoman empires were united into a single Kingdom of Yugoslavia after WWI, then violently fragmented into independent successor states in the 1990s.

stages.label: South Slav lands split among empires

name: Formation of Modern India
summary: The Mughal Empire and a mosaic of regional powers gave way to British rule. Independence in 1947 — with the partition of Pakistan — produced the modern Republic of India.

name: Poland: Partition & Rebirth
summary: The vast Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth was partitioned out of existence by Russia, Prussia and Austria. Poland reappeared after WWI, then had its borders shifted bodily westward after WWII.
